articles.title: COLLABORATING ON APIS IS EASIER WITH POSTMAN
articles.summary: For dev teams relying on APIs, collaboration is crucial. To ship better software, API producers and consumers need to share, discover, and reuse high-quality API assets.

articles.title: THE STORY OF REFORMATTING 100K FILES AT GOOGLE IN 2012
articles.summary: In 2012, Google embarked on a project to reformat all Bazel BUILD files, which was tough because of inconsistent formatting and a variety of developer preferences. A new tool called Buildifier, which strictly enforced a unified style, was developed to automate this process. The rollout was successful - it allowed for better code maintenance and enabled large-scale changes that were previously considered impossible.

articles.title: DEMYSTIFYING INP: NEW TOOLS AND ACTIONABLE INSIGHTS
articles.summary: Interaction to Next Paint (INP) is a Core Web Vital metric that measures the time it takes for a web page to respond to user interaction. It can be optimized by minimizing JavaScript execution time, shortening event handler duration, and reducing the amount of DOM that needs to be re-rendered.

articles.title: HOW GITHUB REBUILT THEIR PUSH PROCESSING SYSTEM
articles.summary: GitHub revamped its code push handling system by transitioning from a single monolithic job to a decoupled architecture using Apache Kafka. This change involved breaking down the original large job into smaller, independent tasks based on dependencies and ownership. They improved reliability by enabling targeted retries and reduced latency by allowing parallel processing.

articles.title: GOOD CODE IS RARELY READ
articles.summary: Good code should prioritize ease of use over readability. It should be intuitive and self-explanatory and shouldn't require much reading to understand its purpose and functionality.

articles.title: GEN AI WILL INCREASE DEMAND FOR SOFTWARE ENGINEERS
articles.summary: This author believes that generative AI will increase demand for software engineers. He compares it to autopilot systems on airplanes which made piloting more accessible and grew the aviation industry, leading to increased demand and higher salaries for pilots. While the specific skillset required for software engineers might change due to generative AI, general skills like communication, problem-solving, and creativity will remain and be even more important.

articles.title: WHY STRUCTURE AND INTERPRETATION OF COMPUTER PROGRAMS MATTERS
articles.summary: The textbook, Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs (SICP), revolutionized introductory computer science education by focusing on big-picture ideas like abstraction and functions as data, rather than just programming language details. It chose Scheme as the teaching language, which allowed students to grasp concepts without getting bogged down in syntax. SICP set a high bar for student expectations, challenging them with complex tasks like building interpreters. The SICP course has been very popular at UC Berkeley and has served well in educating computer science students that do well in industry and graduate school.

articles.title: HTMD
articles.summary: HTMD is an HTML to Markdown converter for Rust. It is fast and has minimal dependencies and a range of options.

articles.title: JSONTREE.JS
articles.summary: JsonTree.js is a lightweight JavaScript library that generates customizable tree views to better visualize JSON data.

articles.title: DGM.JS
articles.summary: DGM.js is an infinite canvas with smart shapes. It supports smart shapes, headless components (React), multi-page support, hand-drawn styles, real-time collaboration, and various import options.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY AI IS LYING ABOUT THEIR USER AGENT
articles.summary: Perplexity AI claims to use a user agent called PerplexityBot, but it actually uses a generic Chrome user agent. It is likely doing this to scrape content from websites that disallow scraping in their robots.txt files.

articles.title: POSTGRES IS ALL YOU NEED, EVEN FOR VECTORS
articles.summary: PostgreSQL with the pgvector extension offers an efficient way to store and query embeddings. It offers simplified querying, data consistency, and better performance compared to using separate databases for relational and vector data.

articles.title: THE ARCHITECTURE BEHIND A ONE-PERSON TECH STARTUP
articles.summary: This post breaks down the setup for a one-person SaaS tech startup, from load balancing to cron job monitoring to payments and subscriptions. The author uses a Kubernetes cluster on AWS, Cloudflare for load balancing and caching, ingress-nginx for routing traffic within the cluster, Flux for automatic deployments, and RDS for database management.

articles.title: REVERSE ENGINEERING A RESTAURANT PAGER SYSTEM
articles.summary: This developer reverse engineered a restaurant pager system by analyzing the radio signals and packet structure and was able to successfully ring pagers with a quick modulator.

articles.title: SQLITE STUDIO
articles.summary: A single binary, single command SQLite database explorer.

articles.title: SIMPLE SABOTAGE FOR SOFTWARE
articles.summary: A CTO could sabotage a company through technology, product, leadership, hiring, and project management decisions, resulting in decreased productivity and ultimately harming the company.

articles.title: 10 YEARS OF VUE: THE PAST AND THE FUTURE BY VUE FOUNDER EVAN YOU (28 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Vue Founder Evan You talks about Vue.js's history, what's new in Vue.js 3, and what's next for it.
